ExternalServiceRegistration.description: Runs Account aggregations — top accounts by revenue, distribution by industry, most recently created, or an overall data-health summary — so an Agentforce agent or MCP client can answer analytics questions about Accounts.
ExternalServiceRegistration.label: AccountAnalytics
ExternalServiceRegistration.schema: openapi: 3.0.0 info: title: AccountAnalytics description: Runs Account aggregations — top accounts by revenue, distribution by industry, most recently created, or an overall data-health summary — so an Agentforce agent or MCP client can answer analytics questions about Accounts. version: '1.0.0' servers: - url: /services/aura-controllers/custom paths: /invoke: post: operationId: invoke summary: Run account analytics description: Runs aggregations over the Account object and returns a human-readable summary. Choose the analytics with mode — top_revenue (largest accounts by AnnualRevenue), by_industry (distribution across industries, or a drill-in when industry is supplied), recent (most recently created), or health_summary (overall data completeness and totals). Results respect the running user sharing and field-level security. x-sfdc: agent: action: publishAsAgentAction: true requestBody: required: true content: application/json: schema: type: object required: - mode properties: mode: title: Mode description: Which analytic to run. One of top_revenue, by_industry, recent, or health_summary. Defaults to health_summary when blank. type: string x-sfdc: agent: action: isDisplayable: true isUserInput: true top_n: title: Top N description: How many rows to return. Used by top_revenue and recent (default 10, capped at 50). type: integer x-sfdc: agent: action: isDisplayable: true isUserInput: true industry: title: Industry Filter description: Optional. Used only by by_industry to drill into a single industry segment instead of returning the full distribution. type: string x-sfdc: agent: action: isDisplayable: true isUserInput: true responses: '200': description: The analytics summary, the mode that ran, and the number of records considered. content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/AnalyticsResult' components: schemas: AnalyticsResult: type: object properties: summary: type: string description: Human-readable summary of the requested analytics. mode: type: string description: The analytics mode that was run. total: type: integer description: The number of records considered for the result.
